entries.statement: In the pinned reference implementation itself, every periphery module reaches the graph only through the read and propose contract, never past it to the kernel's own truth fields.

canonical.method: static-analysis evidence: runs the pinned reference implementation's own import-graph oracle, asserting every periphery import edge satisfies its trust boundary and every flow is supported

entries.statement: Uplift is trust composed with view: a kernel establishes trust once, computed and checkable, a periphery supplies a view fitted to one reader, and the uplift is the composition of the two.

canonical.statement: Uplift is trust composed with view: a kernel establishes trust once, computed and checkable, a periphery supplies a view fitted to one reader, and the uplift is the composition of the two.

entries.statement: A market of views over one shared trust base lets many specialized interfaces compete on how to show the same knowledge while agreeing on the knowledge itself, which a synthesis cannot support because it fuses trust and view into one artifact.

canonical.statement: A market of views over one shared trust base lets many specialized interfaces compete on how to show the same knowledge while agreeing on the knowledge itself, which a synthesis cannot support because it fuses trust and view into one artifact.

entries.statement: The periphery is minimal by design: an existence proof that the interface is cheap to build once the underlying structure is checkable, not a limitation on what could be built.

canonical.statement: The periphery is minimal by design: an existence proof that the interface is cheap to build once the underlying structure is checkable, not a limitation on what could be built.

entries.statement: The contribution of this submission is properly located in the kernel, not in the periphery's thickness; a thicker periphery would misrepresent a protocol's checkable core as a finished product.

canonical.statement: The contribution of this submission is properly located in the kernel, not in the periphery's thickness; a thicker periphery would misrepresent a protocol's checkable core as a finished product.

entries.statement: Knowledge is the invariant left as the model is attenuated: what a claim's dependence on any one knower reduces to as trust in the asserter is subtracted.

canonical.statement: Knowledge is the invariant left as the model is attenuated: what a claim's dependence on any one knower reduces to as trust in the asserter is subtracted.

entries.statement: Verification and convergence are the two forms typing-attenuation takes depending on whether a type self-secures: a proof's attenuation completes to zero, while a measurement's attenuation approaches a floor without reaching it.

canonical.statement: Verification and convergence are the two forms typing-attenuation takes depending on whether a type self-secures: a proof's attenuation completes to zero, while a measurement's attenuation approaches a floor without reaching it.

entries.statement: The ranking/objective perturbation overlay's consequence cascade is computed from the support graph and reproduces the authored cascade fixture, rather than being asserted; the overlay is non-destructive and deterministic across repeated runs.

checking_records.method: test-execution evidence: runs the pinned reference implementation's perturbation oracle over the LHC case, asserting the computed cascade reproduces the authored one and the overlay is non-destructive and deterministic

canonical.method: test-execution evidence: runs the pinned reference implementation's perturbation oracle over the LHC case, asserting the computed cascade reproduces the authored one and the overlay is non-destructive and deterministic

canonical.statement: The ranking/objective perturbation overlay's consequence cascade is computed from the support graph and reproduces the authored cascade fixture, rather than being asserted; the overlay is non-destructive and deterministic across repeated runs.

checking_records.method: test-execution evidence: runs the pinned reference implementation's perturbation oracle over the LHC case, asserting the computed cascade reproduces the authored one and the overlay is non-destructive and deterministic

entries.statement: synthesis: a single reasoner's integrated answer, reading everything and tying each claim to its source, but still one viewpoint's reach.

canonical.statement: synthesis: a single reasoner's integrated answer, reading everything and tying each claim to its source, but still one viewpoint's reach.

entries.statement: typed claim graph: claims labeled by what kind of thing they are and connected to what they rest on, with one rule doing the work.

canonical.statement: typed claim graph: claims labeled by what kind of thing they are and connected to what they rest on, with one rule doing the work.

entries.statement: grounding rule: a claim is as grounded as its weakest necessary support, recomputable by any party trusting no one.

canonical.statement: grounding rule: a claim is as grounded as its weakest necessary support, recomputable by any party trusting no one.

entries.statement: gate: the sole write path, admitting a contribution only if it holds together with what is already there and rechecking every declared grade on every change.

canonical.statement: gate: the sole write path, admitting a contribution only if it holds together with what is already there and rechecking every declared grade on every change.

entries.statement: forum: the contested region of the ordering, tiered from raw through structured, where claims are weighed rather than verified.

canonical.statement: forum: the contested region of the ordering, tiered from raw through structured, where claims are weighed rather than verified.

entries.statement: characterized gap: an open claim carrying its location, its bounds, and the instance that would close it, so an open line is a located object.

canonical.statement: characterized gap: an open claim carrying its location, its bounds, and the instance that would close it, so an open line is a located object.

entries.statement: standing: a claim's grounded grade in the ordering, computed from the structure rather than granted by an authority.

canonical.statement: standing: a claim's grounded grade in the ordering, computed from the structure rather than granted by an authority.

entries.statement: producer: anything that emits claims into the kernel, whether a person, an organization, a model, or a pipeline of these.

canonical.statement: producer: anything that emits claims into the kernel, whether a person, an organization, a model, or a pipeline of these.

entries.statement: crossing: a claim moving from one kernel into another, arriving as the untyped type and losing its local attenuation.

canonical.statement: crossing: a claim moving from one kernel into another, arriving as the untyped type and losing its local attenuation.

entries.statement: untyped type: the one type every schema shares, holding foreign imports and locally untyped claims alike, not a floor, so nothing grounds through it.

canonical.statement: untyped type: the one type every schema shares, holding foreign imports and locally untyped claims alike, not a floor, so nothing grounds through it.

entries.statement: type hash: the deterministic, meaning-sensitive fingerprint of a type bundle, so two kernels that perform the same attenuation pin the same hash.

canonical.statement: type hash: the deterministic, meaning-sensitive fingerprint of a type bundle, so two kernels that perform the same attenuation pin the same hash.

entries.statement: knowledge kernel: a fixed core of typed claims, a gate that checks them, and shared subtrees others fork, opening two markets over one grounding.

canonical.statement: knowledge kernel: a fixed core of typed claims, a gate that checks them, and shared subtrees others fork, opening two markets over one grounding.

entries.statement: typing: subjecting a claim to a type's attenuation, driving its dependence on its author down and staking the agent's standing on the claim holding.

canonical.statement: typing: subjecting a claim to a type's attenuation, driving its dependence on its author down and staking the agent's standing on the claim holding.

entries.statement: declared grade: the grade a claim advertises for itself, admitted only when at or below the earned grade.

canonical.statement: declared grade: the grade a claim advertises for itself, admitted only when at or below the earned grade.

entries.statement: earned grade: the grade a claim actually achieves given what it rests on, computed from the graph alone by the grounding rule.

canonical.statement: earned grade: the grade a claim actually achieves given what it rests on, computed from the graph alone by the grounding rule.

entries.statement: structural attenuation: the kernel's half, driving trust in the producer to zero with respect to structure so a claim's grade recomputes from its cited support by arithmetic anyone can check.

canonical.statement: structural attenuation: the kernel's half, driving trust in the producer to zero with respect to structure so a claim's grade recomputes from its cited support by arithmetic anyone can check.

entries.statement: semantic attenuation: the community's function, judging whether the cited support is true of the world (whether a measurement happened, a checker exists, claimed independence is real), which the kernel makes checkable rather than performs.

canonical.statement: semantic attenuation: the community's function, judging whether the cited support is true of the world (whether a measurement happened, a checker exists, claimed independence is real), which the kernel makes checkable rather than performs.

entries.statement: view: the presentation of a claim fitted to a reader, register, and purpose, separated from the trust that makes the claim hold.

canonical.statement: view: the presentation of a claim fitted to a reader, register, and purpose, separated from the trust that makes the claim hold.

entries.statement: agent: any entity that makes claims and models other claim-makers, a person, an organization, a model, or a pipeline of these.

canonical.statement: agent: any entity that makes claims and models other claim-makers, a person, an organization, a model, or a pipeline of these.

entries.statement: measurement: a floor type where attenuation approaches a floor without reaching zero, so one measurement is testimony and many independent ones are knowledge.

canonical.statement: measurement: a floor type where attenuation approaches a floor without reaching zero, so one measurement is testimony and many independent ones are knowledge.

entries.statement: knowledge: the invariant left as the model is attenuated, knower-independent warranted standing rather than truth.

canonical.statement: knowledge: the invariant left as the model is attenuated, knower-independent warranted standing rather than truth.

entries.statement: In the typed claim graph every claim is independently typed, hashed, and graded from its own support, so a failure is isolable to the claim that failed.

entries.statement: The one financial constraint fixed at protocol level is that penalties trigger on provable fault only, never on being wrong or unpopular.

entries.statement: From inside a single synthesis you cannot tell which parts are the world and which are the viewer, because the viewer is woven through every sentence.

sources.description: the pinned reference implementation's own protocol-spec.md, composition-spec.md, ecosystem-guide.md, and parameters-register.md, the asserting source for this kernel's mechanical claims about what the protocol guarantees

the pinned reference implementation's own epistemic_uplift.md, compost-ledger.md, and trust-and-view.md, unreviewed argumentative essays awaiting the judges' own semantic acceptance, the asserting source for this kernel's evaluative claims
upstream's own vocabulary kernel (corpora/vocabulary/vocabulary.js), the submission's terms as declaration claims each grounding by adoption in the document that defines it
